title: ExperimentPlan v1
description: A declared intent to gain evidence: a gated dispatch performed on purpose. Written at dispatch time, before the outcome is known — it records why the gate is being opened, what evidence is sought, and what risk is accepted. Blocked by default, open by experiment, measured by observation, judged by findings, re-gated by policy.

decision_id.description: The SchedulerDecision this experiment dispatched through. The linkage direction is plan -> decision -> observation.

metric.description: Set on prediction_bias_calibration experiments: which predicted metric is being calibrated.

target_finding_id.description: The belief this experiment is designed to test. Null only for experiments not aimed at an existing finding (e.g. backend_comparison over unexplored ground).

derived_from_candidate.description: candidate_id from experiment_candidates.json, when the experiment was proposed by the projection rather than an operator.

gate_opened.description: The policy rule this dispatch would otherwise be stopped or shaped by. Null for ungated experiment types (e.g. prefer_validation, calibration).

flag.description: The override flag that opened the gate, e.g. experiment_flag.

reason.description: Why this is worth retesting — stated for a human operator.

evidence_sought.description: What evidence would change the target finding, stated concretely enough to judge the result against.

risk_accepted.description: The risk/cost being accepted by opening the gate.
